Machine Learning 101

On this page

  • Objective:
  • 1. Setting up R Packages
  • 2. Reading Data
  • 3. Examining and Cleaning Data
  • 4. Data Dictionary
  • 5. Graphs
  • 6. Predicting Model
  • 7. Prediction using train and test data
  • 8. Summary

Lake Placid Women’s Ironman Triathlon

Linear Regression
Author

Nikhita Purohit

Published

March 17, 2026

The data set looks at female participants in the triathlon Iron-man

Objective:

The motivation for this data analysis is to explore the relationships between swim times, bike times, and run times (in minutes) in order to gain insights into the performance patterns of the athletes. By analyzing these relationships, we can understand the interplay between different segments of the race and potentially identify areas of improvement for athletes.

1. Setting up R Packages

library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggformula)
library(janitor)
library(naniar)
library(visdat)

2. Reading Data

iron <- readr::read_csv("../../data/ironman_lake_placid_female_2022.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

3. Examining and Cleaning Data

dim(iron)
[1] 489  17
str(iron)
spc_tbl_ [489 × 17] (S3: spec_tbl_df/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
 $ Bib          : num [1:489] 3 1 8 5 2 10 7 6 313 15 ...
 $ Name         : chr [1:489] "Sarah True" "Heather Jackson" "Jodie Robertson" "Rachel Zilinskas" ...
 $ Country      : chr [1:489] "United States" "United States" "United States" "United States" ...
 $ Gender       : chr [1:489] "Female" "Female" "Female" "Female" ...
 $ Division     : chr [1:489] "FPRO" "FPRO" "FPRO" "FPRO" ...
 $ Division.Rank: num [1:489] 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 1 9 ...
 $ Overall.Time : num [1:489] 540 556 562 573 575 ...
 $ Overall.Rank : num [1:489] 11 13 16 20 21 28 33 35 39 40 ...
 $ Swim.Time    : num [1:489] 55.6 65.4 62.9 51 58 ...
 $ Swim.Rank    : num [1:489] 28 238 148 10 57 254 58 60 161 134 ...
 $ Bike.Time    : num [1:489] 296 292 305 312 306 ...
 $ Bike.Rank    : num [1:489] 21 18 33 47 35 36 60 23 97 86 ...
 $ Run.Time     : num [1:489] 184 194 188 203 206 ...
 $ Run.Rank     : num [1:489] 7 16 11 30 35 38 41 98 25 31 ...
 $ Finish.Status: chr [1:489] "Finisher" "Finisher" "Finisher" "Finisher" ...
 $ Location     : chr [1:489] "Lake Placid" "Lake Placid" "Lake Placid" "Lake Placid" ...
 $ Year         : num [1:489] 2022 2022 2022 2022 2022 ...
 - attr(*, "spec")=
  .. cols(
  ..   Bib = col_double(),
  ..   Name = col_character(),
  ..   Country = col_character(),
  ..   Gender = col_character(),
  ..   Division = col_character(),
  ..   Division.Rank = col_double(),
  ..   Overall.Time = col_double(),
  ..   Overall.Rank = col_double(),
  ..   Swim.Time = col_double(),
  ..   Swim.Rank = col_double(),
  ..   Bike.Time = col_double(),
  ..   Bike.Rank = col_double(),
  ..   Run.Time = col_double(),
  ..   Run.Rank = col_double(),
  ..   Finish.Status = col_character(),
  ..   Location = col_character(),
  ..   Year = col_double()
  .. )
 - attr(*, "problems")=<externalptr> 
head(iron)
# A tibble: 6 × 17
    Bib Name     Country Gender Division Division.Rank Overall.Time Overall.Rank
  <dbl> <chr>    <chr>   <chr>  <chr>            <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>
1     3 Sarah T… United… Female FPRO                 1         540.           11
2     1 Heather… United… Female FPRO                 2         556.           13
3     8 Jodie R… United… Female FPRO                 3         562.           16
4     5 Rachel … United… Female FPRO                 4         573.           20
5     2 Melanie… Canada  Female FPRO                 5         575.           21
6    10 Angela … United… Female FPRO                 6         586.           28
# ℹ 9 more variables: Swim.Time <dbl>, Swim.Rank <dbl>, Bike.Time <dbl>,
#   Bike.Rank <dbl>, Run.Time <dbl>, Run.Rank <dbl>, Finish.Status <chr>,
#   Location <chr>, Year <dbl>
tail(iron)
# A tibble: 6 × 17
    Bib Name     Country Gender Division Division.Rank Overall.Time Overall.Rank
  <dbl> <chr>    <chr>   <chr>  <chr>            <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>
1  2255 Julia G… United… Female F18-24               5        1014.         1799
2   857 Lauren … United… Female F45-49              86        1017.         1806
3   101 Megan H… United… Female FPC                 NA        1018.         1809
4  1102 Colleen… United… Female F50-54              75        1036.         1814
5   447 Mary Ba… United… Female F50-54              76        1039.         1815
6   448 Kendra … United… Female F50-54              77        1041.         1816
# ℹ 9 more variables: Swim.Time <dbl>, Swim.Rank <dbl>, Bike.Time <dbl>,
#   Bike.Rank <dbl>, Run.Time <dbl>, Run.Rank <dbl>, Finish.Status <chr>,
#   Location <chr>, Year <dbl>
names(iron)
 [1] "Bib"           "Name"          "Country"       "Gender"       
 [5] "Division"      "Division.Rank" "Overall.Time"  "Overall.Rank" 
 [9] "Swim.Time"     "Swim.Rank"     "Bike.Time"     "Bike.Rank"    
[13] "Run.Time"      "Run.Rank"      "Finish.Status" "Location"     
[17] "Year"         
visdat::vis_dat(iron, sort_type = TRUE)
iron_modified <- iron %>% tidyr::drop_na()
visdat::vis_dat(iron_modified, sort_type = TRUE)
iron_modified <- iron %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor)) %>% 
  relocate(where(is.factor))
glimpse(iron_modified)
Rows: 489
Columns: 17
$ Name          <fct> Sarah True, Heather Jackson, Jodie Robertson, Rachel Zil…
$ Country       <fct> United States, United States, United States, United Stat…
$ Gender        <fct> Female, Female, Female, Female, Female, Female, Female, …
$ Division      <fct> FPRO, FPRO, FPRO, FPRO, FPRO, FPRO, FPRO, FPRO, F30-34, …
$ Finish.Status <fct> Finisher, Finisher, Finisher, Finisher, Finisher, Finish…
$ Location      <fct> Lake Placid, Lake Placid, Lake Placid, Lake Placid, Lake…
$ Bib           <dbl> 3, 1, 8, 5, 2, 10, 7, 6, 313, 15, 9, 333, 14, 408, 12, 3…
$ Division.Rank <dbl> 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 1, 9, 10, 1, 11, 2, 12, 1, 2, 13…
$ Overall.Time  <dbl> 540.3667, 556.3833, 562.0333, 572.5500, 574.5333, 585.60…
$ Overall.Rank  <dbl> 11, 13, 16, 20, 21, 28, 33, 35, 39, 40, 51, 67, 70, 74, …
$ Swim.Time     <dbl> 55.60000, 65.36667, 62.91667, 50.95000, 58.05000, 65.833…
$ Swim.Rank     <dbl> 28, 238, 148, 10, 57, 254, 58, 60, 161, 134, 253, 53, 12…
$ Bike.Time     <dbl> 295.5000, 292.3500, 304.5333, 311.8667, 305.5333, 306.03…
$ Bike.Rank     <dbl> 21, 18, 33, 47, 35, 36, 60, 23, 97, 86, 94, 93, 57, 187,…
$ Run.Time      <dbl> 184.1167, 193.8833, 187.6833, 203.3833, 205.7500, 208.13…
$ Run.Rank      <dbl> 7, 16, 11, 30, 35, 38, 41, 98, 25, 31, 52, 113, 149, 43,…
$ Year          <dbl> 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 20…
iron_modified %>%
  DT::datatable(
    style = "default",
    caption = htmltools::tags$caption(
      style = "caption-side: top; text-align: left; color: black; font-size: 100%;", "Lake Placid Women’s Ironman Triathlon Dataset (Clean)"
    ),
    options = list(pageLength = 10, autoWidth = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  DT::formatStyle(
    columns = names(iron_modified),
    fontFamily = "Arial",
    fontSize = "12px",
  )

4. Data Dictionary

Quantitative Data

  1. Bib (int): registration number of each runner used for identification
  2. Division.Rank (int): Within the divisions, the place each runner has obtained over all races
  3. Overall.Time (dbl): The total time it took to complete the Ironman in minutes
  4. Overall.Rank (int): The runner’s finishing place for that particular triathlon
  5. Swim.Time (dbl): The time in minutes it took to complete the swimming portion
  6. Swim.Rank (int): The place the runner finished for the swim portion
  7. Bike.Time (dbl): The time in minutes it took to complete the biking portion
  8. Bike.Rank (int): The place the runner finished for the bike portion
  9. Run.Time (dbl): The time in minutes it took to complete the running portion
  10. Run.Rank (int): The place the runner finished for the running portion
  11. Year (int): The year when the mentioned participant ran

Qualitative Data

  1. Name (fct): The participant’s name
  2. Country (fct): What country the participant is from
  3. Gender (fct): The participant’s gender
  4. Division (fct): The age range or membership a runner is in
  5. Finish.Status (fct): States whether someone completed the Ironman successfully
  6. Location (fct): Where the Ironman takes place
summary(iron_modified)
               Name              Country       Gender       Division 
 Abigail Anderson:  1   United States:413   Female:489   F40-44 :94  
 Aimee Cochran   :  1   Canada       : 64                F45-49 :85  
 Alanna Brown    :  1   Germany      :  2                F50-54 :77  
 Alanna Hollborn :  1   Mexico       :  2                F35-39 :61  
 Alena Storlie   :  1   Austria      :  1                F30-34 :52  
 Alexandra Watt  :  1   Colombia     :  1                F55-59 :44  
 (Other)         :483   (Other)      :  6                (Other):76  
  Finish.Status        Location        Bib       Division.Rank   
 Finisher:489   Lake Placid:489   Min.   :   1   Min.   :  1.00  
                                  1st Qu.: 551   1st Qu.: 13.00  
                                  Median :1078   Median : 30.00  
                                  Mean   :1107   Mean   : 34.25  
                                  3rd Qu.:1626   3rd Qu.: 51.00  
                                  Max.   :2367   Max.   :155.00  
                                                 NA's   :1       
  Overall.Time     Overall.Rank    Swim.Time         Swim.Rank   
 Min.   : 540.4   Min.   :  11   Min.   :  50.95   Min.   :  10  
 1st Qu.: 777.0   1st Qu.: 579   1st Qu.:  72.70   1st Qu.: 603  
 Median : 857.6   Median :1033   Median :  81.47   Median :1183  
 Mean   : 849.3   Mean   :1003   Mean   :  88.10   Mean   :1117  
 3rd Qu.: 937.8   3rd Qu.:1455   3rd Qu.:  89.03   3rd Qu.:1626  
 Max.   :1041.4   Max.   :1816   Max.   :3498.00   Max.   :2106  
                                                                 
   Bike.Time       Bike.Rank       Run.Time        Run.Rank           Year     
 Min.   :292.4   Min.   :  18   Min.   :184.1   Min.   :   7.0   Min.   :2022  
 1st Qu.:392.8   1st Qu.: 726   1st Qu.:288.6   1st Qu.: 518.0   1st Qu.:2022  
 Median :425.2   Median :1187   Median :324.1   Median : 942.0   Median :2022  
 Mean   :424.8   Mean   :1136   Mean   :324.6   Mean   : 942.6   Mean   :2022  
 3rd Qu.:459.5   3rd Qu.:1602   3rd Qu.:366.8   3rd Qu.:1397.0   3rd Qu.:2022  
 Max.   :531.4   Max.   :1995   Max.   :459.4   Max.   :1829.0   Max.   :2022

5. Graphs

iron_modified %>%
  gf_histogram(~ Overall.Time,
               fill = "cyan4",
               bins = 20) %>%
  gf_labs(title = "Ironman Completion Times",
          x = "Time (minutes)",
          y = "Number of People")
iron_modified %>%
  gf_histogram(~ Swim.Time,
               fill = "blue",
               bins = 20) %>%
  gf_labs(title = "Swim Completion Times",
          x = "Time (minutes)",
          y = "Number of People")
iron_modified %>%
  gf_histogram(~ Bike.Time,
               fill = "palevioletred",
               bins = 20) %>%
  gf_labs(title = "Bike Completion Times",
          x = "Time (minutes)",
          y = "Number of People") 
iron_modified %>%
  gf_histogram(~ Run.Time,
               fill = "orange",
               bins = 20) %>%
  gf_labs(title = "Run Completion Times",
          x = "Time (minutes)",
          y = "Number of People")
iron_modified %>%
gf_point(Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time, data = iron_modified, color = 'lightgreen', size = 2) %>%
  gf_smooth(color = 'black') %>% 
  gf_labs(title = "Overall Ironman Time vs Swim Time",
          x = "Swim Time (minutes)",
          y = "Overall Time (minutes)")
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess'
iron_modified %>%
gf_point(Overall.Time ~ Run.Time, data = iron_modified, color = 'lightblue', size = 2) %>%
  gf_smooth(color = 'black') %>% 
  gf_labs(title = "Overall Ironman Time vs Run Time",
          x = "Run Time (minutes)",
          y = "Overall Time (minutes)")
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess'
iron_modified %>%
gf_point(Overall.Time ~ Bike.Time, data = iron_modified, color = 'lightpink', size = 2) %>%
  gf_smooth(color = 'black') %>% 
  gf_labs(title = "Overall Ironman Time vs Bike Time",
          x = "Bike Time (minutes)",
          y = "Overall Time (minutes)")
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess'
iron_modified %>%
gf_point(Run.Time ~ Bike.Time, data = iron_modified, color = 'slategrey', size = 2) %>%
  gf_smooth(color = 'black') %>% 
  gf_labs(title = "Bike Time vs Run Time",
          x = "Bike Time (minutes)",
          y = "Run Time (minutes)")
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess'
iron_modified %>%
gf_point(Run.Time ~ Swim.Time, data = iron_modified, color = 'orange', size = 2) %>%
  gf_smooth(color = 'black') %>% 
  gf_labs(title = "Run Time vs Swim Time",
          x = "Swim Time (minutes)",
          y = "Run Time (minutes)")
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess'
iron_modified %>%
gf_point(Bike.Time ~ Swim.Time, data = iron_modified, color = 'purple', size = 2) %>%
  gf_smooth(color = 'black') %>% 
  gf_labs(title = "Bike Time vs Swim Time",
          x = "Swim Time (minutes)",
          y = "Bike Time (minutes)")
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess'
bike_vs_overall_lm <- lm(Overall.Time ~ Bike.Time, data = iron_modified)
bike_vs_overall_lm

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Bike.Time, data = iron_modified)

Coefficients:
(Intercept)    Bike.Time  
     -2.757        2.006  
iron_modified %>%
  gf_point(Overall.Time ~ Bike.Time, color = 'maroon') %>%
  gf_lm(color = "black")%>%
  gf_labs(title = "Overall Time vs Bike Time",
          x = "Bike Time (minutes)",
          y = "Overall Time (minutes)")
summary(bike_vs_overall_lm)

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Bike.Time, data = iron_modified)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-119.026  -29.566   -3.339   28.606  129.259 

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) -2.75703   16.42577  -0.168    0.867    
Bike.Time    2.00595    0.03842  52.205   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 40.95 on 487 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.8484,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.8481 
F-statistic:  2725 on 1 and 487 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
swim_vs_overall_lm <- lm(Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time, data = iron_modified)
swim_vs_overall_lm

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time, data = iron_modified)

Coefficients:
(Intercept)    Swim.Time  
  847.34890      0.02194  
iron_modified %>%
  gf_point(Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time, color = 'hotpink') %>%
  gf_lm(color = "black")%>%
  gf_labs(title = "Overall Time vs Swim Time",
          x = "Swim Time (minutes)",
          y = "Overall Time (minutes)")
summary(swim_vs_overall_lm)

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time, data = iron_modified)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-308.202  -73.236    8.459   88.177  192.227 

Coefficients:
             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) 847.34890    5.46926 154.929   <2e-16 ***
Swim.Time     0.02194    0.03071   0.715    0.475    
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 105.1 on 487 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.001047,  Adjusted R-squared:  -0.001004 
F-statistic: 0.5106 on 1 and 487 DF,  p-value: 0.4752
run_vs_overall_lm <- lm(Overall.Time ~ Run.Time, data = iron_modified)
run_vs_overall_lm

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Run.Time, data = iron_modified)

Coefficients:
(Intercept)     Run.Time  
    268.093        1.791  
iron_modified %>%
  gf_point(Overall.Time ~ Run.Time, color = 'turquoise') %>%
  gf_lm(color = "black")%>%
  gf_labs(title = "Overall Time vs Run Time",
          x = "Run Time (minutes)",
          y = "Overall Time (minutes)")
summary(run_vs_overall_lm)

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Run.Time, data = iron_modified)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-112.058  -30.985   -1.286   29.649  123.760 

Coefficients:
             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) 268.09343   12.05663   22.24   <2e-16 ***
Run.Time      1.79059    0.03665   48.85   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 43.29 on 487 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.8305,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.8302 
F-statistic:  2387 on 1 and 487 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

6. Predicting Model

test = data.frame(Bike.Time = 300)

predict(bike_vs_overall_lm, newdata = test)
       1 
599.0278 
test = data.frame(Run.Time = 250)

predict(run_vs_overall_lm, newdata = test)
       1 
715.7404

7. Prediction using train and test data

rows <- nrow(iron_modified)
rows
[1] 489
0.75 * rows
[1] 366.75
train_rows <- sample(1:nrow(iron_modified), 367)
train_data = iron_modified[train_rows, ]
test_data = iron_modified[-train_rows, ]
table(test_data$Country)

       Austria         Canada       Colombia        Czechia        Germany 
             1             27              1              0              1 
       Ireland          Italy         Mexico    Netherlands    Philippines 
             1              1              0              1              0 
United Kingdom  United States 
             0             89 
table(iron_modified$Country)

       Austria         Canada       Colombia        Czechia        Germany 
             1             64              1              1              2 
       Ireland          Italy         Mexico    Netherlands    Philippines 
             1              1              2              1              1 
United Kingdom  United States 
             1            413 
table(iron_modified$Finish.Status)

Finisher 
     489 
# Yayy
prop.table(table(iron_modified$Country))

       Austria         Canada       Colombia        Czechia        Germany 
    0.00204499     0.13087935     0.00204499     0.00204499     0.00408998 
       Ireland          Italy         Mexico    Netherlands    Philippines 
    0.00204499     0.00204499     0.00408998     0.00204499     0.00204499 
United Kingdom  United States 
    0.00204499     0.84458078
  1. swim time -> overall time
swim_lm <- lm(Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time,
                  data = train_data)
summary(swim_lm)

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time, data = train_data)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-208.327  -56.886   -4.255   61.208  270.853 

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) 409.4763    31.1133   13.16   <2e-16 ***
Swim.Time     5.4345     0.3778   14.38   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 81.54 on 365 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.3618,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.3601 
F-statistic: 206.9 on 1 and 365 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
predicted_values1 <- predict(swim_lm, newdata = test_data)
  1. bike time -> overall time
bike_lm <- lm(Overall.Time ~ Bike.Time,
              data = train_data)
summary(bike_lm)

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Bike.Time, data = train_data)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-100.875  -30.530   -3.661   31.226  129.539 

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) 10.21302   19.28235    0.53    0.597    
Bike.Time    1.97599    0.04494   43.97   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 40.68 on 365 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.8412,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.8407 
F-statistic:  1933 on 1 and 365 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
predicted_values2 = predict(bike_lm, newdata = test_data)
  1. run time -> overall time
run_lm <- lm(Overall.Time ~ Run.Time,
             data = train_data)
summary(run_lm)

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Run.Time, data = train_data)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-111.916  -29.942   -0.554   30.764  121.693 

Coefficients:
             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) 274.69658   14.34931   19.14   <2e-16 ***
Run.Time      1.77402    0.04348   40.80   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 43.28 on 365 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.8202,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.8197 
F-statistic:  1665 on 1 and 365 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
predicted_values3 <- predict(run_lm, newdata = test_data)
  1. swim + bike -> overall
sb_lm <- lm(Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time + Bike.Time,
            data = train_data)
summary(sb_lm)

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time + Bike.Time, data = train_data)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-100.903  -27.556   -2.305   28.500  119.258 

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) -19.9299    19.3403   -1.03    0.303    
Swim.Time     1.2016     0.2181    5.51 6.79e-08 ***
Bike.Time     1.8168     0.0520   34.94  < 2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 39.13 on 364 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.8534,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.8526 
F-statistic:  1059 on 2 and 364 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
predicted_values4 <- predict(sb_lm, newdata = test_data)
  1. bike + run -> overall
br_lm <- lm(Overall.Time ~ Bike.Time + Run.Time,
            data = train_data)
summary(br_lm)

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Bike.Time + Run.Time, data = train_data)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-33.034  -7.133  -0.722   6.222  37.879 

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)  4.24455    5.39089   0.787    0.432    
Bike.Time    1.20619    0.01719  70.178   <2e-16 ***
Run.Time     1.02559    0.01563  65.628   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 11.37 on 364 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.9876,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.9876 
F-statistic: 1.452e+04 on 2 and 364 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
predicted_values5 <- predict(br_lm, newdata = test_data)
  1. swim + run -> overall
sr_lm <- lm(Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time + Run.Time,
            data = train_data)
summary(sr_lm)

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time + Run.Time, data = train_data)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-98.361 -23.140  -1.639  21.330 151.497 

Coefficients:
             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) 139.92416   14.69808    9.52   <2e-16 ***
Swim.Time     2.54206    0.17451   14.57   <2e-16 ***
Run.Time      1.55119    0.03784   41.00   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 34.45 on 364 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.8864,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.8858 
F-statistic:  1420 on 2 and 364 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
predicted_values6 <- predict(sr_lm, newdata = test_data)
  1. swim + bike + run -> overall
sbr_lm <- lm(Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time + Bike.Time + Run.Time,
             data = train_data)
summary(sbr_lm)

Call:
lm(formula = Overall.Time ~ Swim.Time + Bike.Time + Run.Time, 
    data = train_data)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-14.395  -3.311  -0.582   2.664  63.324 

Coefficients:
              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) -21.568997   2.941369  -7.333 1.48e-12 ***
Swim.Time     1.031477   0.033193  31.075  < 2e-16 ***
Bike.Time     1.077424   0.009905 108.780  < 2e-16 ***
Run.Time      1.015076   0.008186 123.994  < 2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 5.952 on 363 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.9966,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.9966 
F-statistic: 3.566e+04 on 3 and 363 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
predicted_values7 <- predict(sbr_lm, newdata = test_data)

Finding RMSE for each linear model

sqrt(mean((predicted_values1-test_data$Overall.Time)^2))
[1] 1686.131
sqrt(mean((predicted_values2-test_data$Overall.Time)^2))
[1] 41.84342
sqrt(mean((predicted_values3-test_data$Overall.Time)^2))
[1] 43.42223
sqrt(mean((predicted_values4-test_data$Overall.Time)^2))
[1] 378.0079
sqrt(mean((predicted_values5-test_data$Overall.Time)^2))
[1] 10.5041
sqrt(mean((predicted_values6-test_data$Overall.Time)^2))
[1] 787.2074
sqrt(mean((predicted_values7-test_data$Overall.Time)^2))
[1] 320.9703

Therefore, we can conclude that the fifth model [ bike + run -> overall ] is the most successful linear model because it has the lowest RMSE.

8. Summary

The Ironman triathlon is held in this particular order- swimming, biking, and finally running. The order as well as the distance of these sports highly affects the data. In this dataset, the graphs for run completion times and bike completion times are normal data distributions. However, the graph for swim completion times is very odd. Almost every participant has completed the swim around the same time. On checking the correlation of different attributes, I found that the relation between [run time and overall time] and [bike time and overall time] were linear, so I used them to make two prediction models. Swim time was not so helpful. Finally, the fifth model [ bike + run -> overall ] was the most successful linear model because it has the lowest RMSE.
